---
pagetitle: "JSC370 Final Project — Carbon Dioxide Emissions per Capita, 1990–2023"
title: ""
toc: false
page-layout: full
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <style>
      body { /* tag the home page so the global CSS can scope hero overrides */ }
    </style>
    <script>
      window.MathJax = {
        tex: { inlineMath: [['$', '$'], ['\\(', '\\)']] },
        svg: { fontCache: 'global' }
      };
    </script>
    <script async src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mathjax@3/es5/tex-svg.js"></script>
include-after-body:
  text: |
    <script>document.body.classList.add('home-page');</script>
---

```{=html}
<section class="hero">
  <span class="eyebrow">JSC370 · Winter 2026 · University of Toronto</span>
  <h1>Carbon Dioxide Emissions per Capita, 1990&ndash;2023</h1>
  <p class="lede">How economic, energy, and land-use factors explain cross-country differences.</p>
  <p class="byline"><strong>Yi Fan (Eric) Wang</strong> &nbsp;·&nbsp; April 26, 2026</p>
  <div class="cta-row">
    <a class="btn-primary" href="report.html">Read the Final Report <span class="arrow">→</span></a>
    <a class="btn-secondary" href="viz.html">Explore Interactive Visuals</a>
    <a class="btn-secondary" href="https://github.com/EricWcr7/JSC370-2026S-Final-Project" target="_blank" rel="noopener">GitHub Repository <span class="arrow">↗</span></a>
    <a class="btn-ghost" href="report.pdf">Download Final Report PDF <span class="arrow down">↓</span></a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="section section-bleed">
  <div class="section-inner">
    <span class="section-label">At a glance</span>
    <div class="stat-strip">
      <div class="stat-card">
        <span class="stat-num">~7,400</span>
        <span class="stat-label">Country–year observations</span>
      </div>
      <div class="stat-card">
        <span class="stat-num">6</span>
        <span class="stat-label">World Bank indicators</span>
      </div>
      <div class="stat-card">
        <span class="stat-num">XGBoost</span>
        <span class="stat-label">Final best model</span>
      </div>
      <div class="stat-card">
        <span class="stat-num">GDP / capita</span>
        <span class="stat-label">Strongest predictor</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="section">
  <span class="section-label">The story</span>
  <div class="section-prose">
    <p>Climate change is one of the major global challenges, and it is largely driven by carbon dioxide emissions from human activities. Those emissions are not evenly distributed across countries: countries at different stages of economic development rely on different energy sources, follow different urbanization paths, and face different land-use pressures, all of which leave distinct imprints on per-capita emissions.</p>
    <p>This project uses a <strong>34-year panel of every country</strong> reporting to the World Bank to map how those imprints have evolved &mdash; and to ask which factors actually drive cross-country differences once everything else is held equal. Six indicators carry the analysis: carbon dioxide emissions per capita as the outcome, with GDP per capita, fossil-fuel electricity share, renewable energy share, forest area, and urban population share as candidate predictors, plus the calendar year as a temporal control.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="callout-question callout-horizontal" role="note">
    <span class="label">Research question</span>
    <p>How have carbon dioxide emissions per capita evolved across countries from 1990 to 2023, and which economic, energy, and land-use factors explain and predict these differences?</p>
  </div>
  <div class="section-prose">
    <p>The <a href="midterm.html"><strong>Midterm Report</strong></a> built the data pipeline from the World Bank Open Data API, cleaned 7,378 country-year records down to 5,499 complete cases, and laid the explanatory groundwork with an <strong>OLS regression</strong> (adjusted $R^2$ approximately 0.66) and a <strong>Generalized Additive Model</strong> (pseudo $R^2$ approximately 0.73). The GAM's significantly nonlinear smooth terms made it clear that a strictly linear story was leaving variance on the table.</p>
    <p>The <strong>Final Report</strong> picks up from there. It keeps the same panel and the same predictor set so the two reports stay directly comparable, then swaps in two flexible tree-based models &mdash; a pruned <strong>Decision Tree</strong> as an interpretable baseline and a tuned <strong>XGBoost Regressor</strong> as the flexible predictor &mdash; with country-grouped train/validation/test splits to avoid country-level leakage. The result is both better predictive performance and a sharper, gain-based picture of which predictors actually do the work.</p>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="section">
  <span class="section-label">Data sources</span>
  <h2>World Bank Open Data API</h2>
  <p class="section-prose-lead">All six indicators come from the <a href="https://data.worldbank.org/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">World Bank Open Data API</a> (WDI), pulled live for 1990&ndash;2023 across every country reporting. After dropping regional aggregates and standardizing missing markers, the panel covers <strong>196 countries</strong> with <strong>7,378 country&ndash;year rows</strong>, which the modelling stage filters to <strong>5,499 complete cases</strong>.</p>
  <div class="data-table-wrap">
    <table class="data-table">
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th>Indicator</th>
          <th>WDI code</th>
          <th>Unit</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
        <tr><td>CO<sub>2</sub> emissions per capita <span class="role">outcome</span></td><td><code>EN.GHG.CO2.PC.CE.AR5</code></td><td>metric tons / capita</td></tr>
        <tr><td>GDP per capita</td><td><code>NY.GDP.PCAP.KD</code></td><td>constant 2015 USD / person</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Fossil-fuel electricity share</td><td><code>EG.ELC.FOSL.ZS</code></td><td>% of electricity</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Renewable energy share</td><td><code>EG.FEC.RNEW.ZS</code></td><td>% of final energy</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Forest area</td><td><code>AG.LND.FRST.ZS</code></td><td>% of land area</td></tr>
        <tr><td>Urban population share</td><td><code>SP.URB.TOTL.IN.ZS</code></td><td>% of population</td></tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="section">
  <span class="section-label">Key findings</span>
  <h2>Three takeaways</h2>
  <div class="findings-grid">
    <article class="finding-card">
      <span class="find-label">Trajectory</span>
      <h3>Emissions peaked in the early 2010s</h3>
      <p>Global per-capita emissions rose through the 2000s, peaked around 2012, then declined &mdash; with a sharp dip in 2020 from the COVID slowdown.</p>
    </article>
    <article class="finding-card">
      <span class="find-label">Best model</span>
      <h3>Tuned XGBoost wins on every metric</h3>
      <p>The tuned XGBoost beats the pruned Decision Tree and an untuned XGBoost baseline on R², RMSE, and MAE on the held-out test set.</p>
    </article>
    <article class="finding-card">
      <span class="find-label">Top driver</span>
      <h3>Log GDP per capita dominates</h3>
      <p>Followed by renewable energy share and urban population share. Forest area, fossil-fuel electricity share, and the calendar year contribute the least.</p>
    </article>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="section">
  <span class="section-label">Model comparison</span>
  <h2>Test performance across models</h2>
  <p class="section-prose-lead">All three models are evaluated on the same held-out test set of unseen countries, using country-grouped train/validation/test splits to prevent country-level leakage. Metrics are on log carbon dioxide emissions per capita.</p>
  <div class="data-table-wrap">
    <table class="data-table model-table">
      <thead>
        <tr>
          <th>Model</th>
          <th>Test R²</th>
          <th>Test RMSE</th>
          <th>Test MAE</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody>
        <tr>
          <td>Decision Tree (pruned)</td>
          <td>0.706</td>
          <td>0.558</td>
          <td>0.374</td>
        </tr>
        <tr class="is-best">
          <td>XGBoost (tuned) <span class="role">best</span></td>
          <td>0.722</td>
          <td>0.543</td>
          <td>0.359</td>
        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
  </div>
  <p class="table-note">Higher R² is better; lower RMSE and MAE are better. The tuned XGBoost wins on every metric &mdash; see <a href="report.html#model-comparison">the report</a> for full hyperparameters and importance plots.</p>
</section>

<section class="section">
  <span class="section-label">What's on this site</span>
  <h2>Four ways to dive in</h2>
  <div class="site-grid site-grid-4">
    <a class="site-card" href="report.html">
      <span class="site-kind">Final Report</span>
      <h3>Full written analysis</h3>
      <p>Introduction, methods, results, and conclusions. Available as <strong>HTML</strong> and as a downloadable <strong>PDF</strong>.</p>
    </a>
    <a class="site-card" href="midterm.html">
      <span class="site-kind">Midterm Report</span>
      <h3>The earlier OLS &amp; GAM analysis</h3>
      <p>Background context — the midterm-stage analysis (OLS regression and GAM) that this <strong>Final Report</strong> extends. <strong>HTML only.</strong></p>
    </a>
    <a class="site-card" href="viz.html">
      <span class="site-kind">Interactive</span>
      <h3>Three plotly figures</h3>
      <p>An animated world CO₂ map, a histogram by decade, and a CO₂-vs-GDP scatter by continent &mdash; all interactive.</p>
    </a>
    <a class="site-card" href="https://github.com/EricWcr7/JSC370-2026S-Final-Project" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
      <span class="site-kind">Code</span>
      <h3>GitHub repository</h3>
      <p>Source <code>.qmd</code> files, the data pipeline, all figures, and the rendered website &mdash; fully reproducible.</p>
    </a>
  </div>
</section>

<section class="section">
  <span class="section-label">Methodology</span>
  <h2>How the analysis flows</h2>
  <div class="method-flow">
    <span class="method-step">Data</span>
    <span class="method-arrow">→</span>
    <span class="method-step">Cleaning</span>
    <span class="method-arrow">→</span>
    <span class="method-step">EDA</span>
    <span class="method-arrow">→</span>
    <span class="method-step is-primary">Decision Tree + XGBoost</span>
    <span class="method-arrow">→</span>
    <span class="method-step">Evaluation</span>
  </div>
</section>

```
